checks.finding: The requirements identify the operands, preservation constraints, verification behavior, and observable outcomes needed for implementation and verification.
The goals, non-goals, workflows, and acceptance criteria consistently preserve the existing consent rule while correcting the shell data boundary.
The acceptance surface covers valid signatures, tampering, adversarial operands, independently invalid nonce/TTL/repository conditions, no-output denial behavior, and both runtimes.
The stated runtime and parity assumptions are bounded by the existing implementations and do not leave a scope or validation decision unresolved.
The specification retains explicit SDD_SUDO consent, requires all validation conditions, and preserves default denial before bundle creation or external-send paths.
Each required behavior has a mapped, observable planned acceptance test, with isolated fixtures and no secrets or network dependency.
